channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Anthropic buys $30 billion of Azure cloud capability - and nets $15 billion investment from Microsoft and Nvidia in return
item.description: Anthropic purchases large Azure compute capacity, gains multi-cloud deployment for Claude models, and secures major investments from Microsoft and Nvidia partners.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: Trump is drafting an executive order to stop state-level AI regulation. Here’s what to know
item.description: President Donald Trump is considering pressuring states to stop regulating artificial intelligence in a draft executive order obtained Thursday by The Associated Press, as some in Congress also consider whether to temporarily block states from regulating AI.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Waymo gets permission to expand robotaxi service to East Bay, North Bay, Sacramento
item.description: Earlier this month, Waymo began offering driverless robotaxi service on Peninsula freeways, and to and from Mineta San Jose International Airport.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Black Friday scams are everywhere now – here are the 5 best ways to stay safe
item.description: Here are 5 ways to keep fraudsters at bay this Black Friday and ensure your money stays out of scammers’ reach.
item.dc:creator: Alex Blake, TechRadar

item.title: Amazon starts Prime payouts from $2.5 billion settlement – here's how the refunds work and when you might get one
item.description: Amazon's started paying out $1.5 billion of its record-breaking settlement with the FTC which is earmarked to refund Prime customers.
item.dc:creator: Darren Allan, TechRadar

item.title: Comet AI browser lands on Android
item.description: Perplexity's Comet browser brings smart, voice-enabled AI help to mobile browsing, aiming to turn every tab into a conversation.
item.dc:creator: Eric Hal Schwartz, TechRadar

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Fears of AI bubble ease
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mullvad VPN adds ultra-fast obfuscation to beat WireGuard blocking
item.description: Mullvad VPN adds Lightweight WireGuard Obfuscation (LWO), a new ultra-light obfuscation protocol designed to hide WireGuard traffic and bypass censorship with almost no speed loss.
item.dc:creator: Monica J. White, TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft has yet to ignite enthusiasm for agentic AI
item.description: It’s the year of the agentic enterprise, according to Microsoft, which at its Ignite show this week positioned autonomous workflows and reasoning-driven automation as the almost-inevitable path that enterprises must eventually take.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Meta enters power trading to support its AI energy needs
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. is venturing into the complex world of electricity trading, betting it can accelerate the construction of new US power plants that are vital to its artificial intelligence ambitions.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: X was down again– here's how its latest outage played out
item.description: X suffered a major blip again this week, with thousands reporting that the social media site was down in the both the US and UK –but it seems to have recovered.
item.dc:creator: Mark Wilson, TechRadar

item.title: Fitbit's new AI tool wants to take the stress out of your next doctor's visit – and I have some serious questions
item.description: Fitbit's new Plan for Care feature lets you chat through your symptoms with AI – what could possibly go wrong?
item.dc:creator: David Nield, TechRadar

item.title: France will investigate Musk’s Grok chatbot after Holocaust denial claims
item.description: France's government is taking action against Elon Musk's AI chatbot, Grok, after it generated posts questioning the use of gas chambers at Auschwitz.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Adamson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Singin’ the Agentic Windows blues
item.description: Once upon a time, when you ran Windows on your desktop, it was your desktop. Oh, the IT department might have called the shots on how much you could do with it, but you could write what you needed to, and it was all kept nicely on your PC or your choice of network drive. Those days are long gone.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: OpenAI and Taiwan's Foxconn to partner in AI hardware design and manufacturing in the US
item.description: OpenAI has announced a partnership with Taiwan's Foxconn to design and make equipment for AI data centers in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Chan Ho-Him And Taijing Wu, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Shopping for toys this holiday season? Avoid the AI ones, groups warn parents
item.description: They’re cute, even cuddly, and promise learning and companionship—but artificial intelligence toys are not safe for kids, according to children’s and consumer advocacy groups urging parents not to buy them during the holiday season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: How pairing SAST with AI dramatically reduces false positives in code security
item.description: The promise of static application security testing (SAST) has always been the “shift-left” dream, catching vulnerabilities before they ever hit production. But for too long, that promise has been undermined by a frustrating reality with an overwhelming volume of alerts and high false-positive rates.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft Ignite 2025
item.description: Microsoft Ignite 2025 runs November 18-21, 2025, in San Francisco, with an optional pre-day on November 17. Can’t make it to the Moscone Center in San Francisco? No problem. It’s a hybrid event, and you can register to attend Ignite (for free) virtually here.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How IT leaders can build successful AI strategies
item.description: The AI gold rush these days is littered with abandoned enterprise projects, with humans — not the technology itself — being blamed for high failure rates of AI projects. Recent data indicates that stagnant AI projects were often the result of poor vision, mismanagement, and a lack of resources.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Azure HorizonDB: Microsoft goes big with PostgreSQL
item.description: Enterprises need data, and data needs to be stored, with a flexible, portable environment that scales from developers’ laptops to global clouds. That storage also needs to be able to run on any OS and any cloud without breaking the bank. There aren’t many options.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft rolls out Agent 365 ‘control plane’ for AI agents
item.description: Microsoft Agent 365 has been introduced as a control plane to help organizations deploy and manage AI agents at scale. Unveiled November 18, Agent 365 is available through Microsoft’s Frontier program for early access to AI technologies.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft drops M365 Copilot price for SMBs, upgrades free Copilot Chat
item.description: Microsoft will reduce the price of Microsoft 365 Copilot for small and mid-sized firms beginning next month. As of Dec. 1, 2025, Microsoft 365 Copilot for Business will cost $21 per user, per month for customers with any Microsoft 365 Business plan.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: When a cyberattack hits, breakdowns inside the organization may be as dangerous as the hackers themselves
item.description: "There's so much focus on threat actors. There's a cost to that, and the cost is that we forget about our own people," Josh Ferenczi said. The post When a cyberattack hits, breakdowns inside the organization may be as dangerous as the hackers themselves first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: Building a scalable document management system: Lessons from separating metadata and content
item.description: When I was tasked with modernizing our enterprise document management system, I knew the stakes were high. We were managing millions of documents, and our legacy architecture was buckling under the load.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A developer’s guide to avoiding the brambles
item.description: Software development is fraught with peril. For any development task, there are a million ways to do it wrong, and the one correct way is a small, narrow trail that winds quietly through the forest. It takes experience to know the path and to avoid the dangerous pitfalls along the way.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google unveils Gemini 3 AI model, Antigravity agentic development tool
item.description: Google has introduced the Gemini 3 AI model, an update of Gemini with improved visual reasoning, and Google Antigravity, an agentic development platform for AI-assisted software development. Both were announced on November 18.
item.dc:creator: Info World
